steps.title: Find the quantity clue
steps.content: The dash after the blank introduces an explanatory clause: “as many different interpretations as there are people to listen to them.” This clause signals that the blank must describe a large, uncountable number of meanings.

steps.title: Eliminate three choices, confirm one
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘superficial’: Means shallow or lacking depth. The passage presents Guraieb’s music as rich with valid interpretations, not as shallow. Violated rule: word meaning contradicts the passage’s positive framing. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘myriad’: Means countless or very many. “Myriad meanings” is confirmed directly by the dash clause describing as many interpretations as there are listeners. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘deficient’: Means lacking or inadequate. The passage says interpretations are hard to call wrong, indicating the meanings are plentiful, not insufficient. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘untenable’: Means indefensible or unsustainable. The passage states a listener’s interpretation is “difficult to justly claim” is wrong, so calling the meanings untenable is the opposite of the passage’s point. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
